reviews.title: It’s got potential
reviews.body: Love the idea, but every time I’ve used it the hose comes of the head.You then have to take it apart and reassemble it.Not very convenient when you are mid shower and covered with soap.I’d give it 4 or 5 stars if it was built better.Save you money or find a unit that’s better engineered.

reviews.title: Great device ... while it worked. WARNING! Major quality issues!
reviews.body: We used it several times and it was great ... while it lasted. However, the cable right at the plug that connects to the battery has developed a false connection. When I moved the showerhead while bathing, sometimes it would cut off. this got worse and worse, and tonight I realized that it was due to a bad connection in the plug that connects to the battery. Now I can't get it to turn on.They will only pay for $20 of return postage, but since I am in Mexico that makes it prohibitively expensive. Great concept. Crappy application. Sadly, it looks like a waste of $60 for me. I cannot recommend sure to long-term quality issues.I'll try contacting Kedsum customer service to see if they make it right, will edit if they do.

reviews.title: Pump died after first use.
reviews.body: The pump stopped working after the first use.  I tested both batteries with a volt meter and they are fully charged.

reviews.title: Horrible
reviews.body: Im so upset , recieved it today and does not work. As you can see , it's properly assembled ith O rings in place and fully submerged in water! Idk if I just got a defective one or what, but I'm for sure returning it tmrw. I had such high hopes for it 😡😡😡😡

reviews.title: Parts would not fit together - If Zero Stars were an option this would get it
reviews.body: Was excited to get the camp shower but right out of the box the hose connection to the sprayer would not connect.  The design of the connection wasn't long enough to start threading onto the sprayer.In addition, the box arrived and it was clear this was shipped to someone before me because the adhesive tabs on the box were already coming off and looked like box had been opened previously.

reviews.title: Not good for washing dogs. Great for about an hour and then broke.
reviews.body: Great for about an hour up until it stopped working  randomly. It sounds like it’s stuck now and we have tried for about an hour to get it to run again. Not a good solution for washing your dog.

reviews.title: It did not work at all !!! very disappointed
reviews.body: One Battery did not charge at all and the other worked but the system as a whole did not work at all.

reviews.title: Waste of money
reviews.body: Why would they make a shower where the battery is not waterproof? Because they want you to have to buy another one because it won't last! Just another example of Chinese garbage! Don't waste your money on this buy one that has the battery built in the pump already. I had to give it at least one star otherwise it would have got no stars

reviews.title: Did not work
reviews.body: Hello I bought this portable shower with a great expectations. I even tried right after i purchase. But when i went to camping suddenly it stopped working. Battery didn’t worked at all even i charged fully.

reviews.title: Nice for the two times I was able to use it.
reviews.body: It was great, in the beginning. I used it for a total of 2 trips and the first trip went flawlessly. The second however, the batteries wouldn't hold a charge, the pump wasnt working properly, the thing just became a disaster. If you only need it for one or two times great, otherwise look elsewhere.

reviews.title: This is complete junk once batteries stop working!!
reviews.body: Very, no, extremely hard to insert pump attachment into battery & turning it on iis damn near impossible & then it only last 5-6 seconds before shutting down & you start all over again!!Not worthy of the junk drawer!!Batteries quit working right after warranty is over!No response from manufacturer

reviews.title: Batteries die or pump
reviews.body: After about 10 uses the batteries, the 2 lithium recieved with this product the shower head NO LONGER SUPPORTS OPERATION OF THE PUMP. Will reach out to support, will update experience and if the replacement maintains.

reviews.title: Doesn't actually heat water
reviews.body: I am really disappointed because I researched many camping showers, and I really wanted this one to work. Unfortunately though the pump does pump water, the water is not hot. I bought this for winter camping.

reviews.title: Complete waste of money
reviews.body: The pump was not strong enough to pump water through the hose. Absolutely useless.Wish I could give it zero stars

reviews.title: Don't buy this
reviews.body: Used it ones and after that, the motor doesn't work  anymore. Not sure if its the rechargeable battery or the motor. Not worth buying this crap.

reviews.title: Motor does not work !
reviews.body: Even after charging battery can not get the motor to turn on haven’t had the chance to use it so I pasted my return window definitely not worth the money

reviews.title: Broke
reviews.body: It stopped working after three uses

reviews.title: Does not work
reviews.body: Have purchased two of these over last 2 years. Both sets have failed. First set worked for a few times, then stopped working when both USB charged batteries will no longer charge and run pump.Second brand new set ordered last summer, first use last week, only one of the two new batteries will charge, and will not power pump even when charged!DO NOTBUY! Zodi brand better!

reviews.title: Junk
reviews.body: I bought this because I have a huge sit down shower and no retractable shower head to clean the shower. In theory this portable shower is perfect to spray down the shower before and after applying cleaning products. However, this thing worked like dog crap. I can't speak for the shower head as I did not use it at all. I only used the spray gun which seemed to work literally whenever it felt like it. I filled up my bucket a couple times and when I pressed the trigger, sometimes it would work and sometimes absolute no water would come out. I did many things like recheck the gaskets, let the pump sit for a couple seconds and it was all useless in the end. Because this was so unreliable, I went back to stepping in the shower barefoot and using a 5 gallon bucket to swish water around to rinse off clorox and whatever else used.My recommendation is don't waste your money on this product.

reviews.title: Both Battery Packs DOA
reviews.body: Both battery packs dead and will not charge. Not worth the high price. Also - for those who may not know -  the white [sink type] sprayer is pointless. The tiny pump cannot handle the back pressure if the sprayer isn't constantly spraying water out while the pump is running.

reviews.title: Does not work
reviews.body: Very disappointed with this.  I brought it camping with me and it did not operate properly.  I plugged in the fully charged battery and the moto turned on, but it would not suck any water.  It is now passed the return window so I am out $40.  Do not buy.

reviews.title: Works for Only 2 Times
reviews.body: Save yourself a headache.  This item worked 2 times and it died.  Batteries were fully charged and were kept dry.The pump worked intermittently during the second time..... and then it died completely.

reviews.title: Stopped working in the middle of a shower
reviews.body: This barely warmed up the water and the shower nozzle stopped spraying water 5 minutes into a shower. The batter was still running but no water came out. Bucket was completely full of water. Everything was submerged. Still no water. Don’t waste your money

reviews.title: Arrived Not Working
reviews.body: It's a dud. I can hear it turn on but it does not pump any water. Should have gone for a manual pump for this reason

reviews.title: Dont waste your money
reviews.body: The pump isnt powerful enough to get it through the entire length of the hose.  If you are over 3 feet tall, you will have a hard time using it.  Spend a little more and get one that actually works

reviews.title: HORRIBLE DESIGN AND DOES NOT HAVE ADEQUATE SUCTION AT ALL!!!!!!!!!!!
reviews.body: I have purchased MANY different designs of USB rechargeable showers and thought I would give this kind with 2 external batteries a try after the Ivation brand gave up the ghost after on a couple of uses. I had High hopes for a solution to the needs of missionaries and had hoped to send these as gifts after trying one out myself. Instructionns were fine, charging was fine, but the device did not even have enough suction to draw the water out of a pail. It would not even suck the water. All fittings were inspected for secure connections and despite flawless installation, the device simply did not work at all. I guess we are back to a solar bag hung on a tree...

reviews.title: Expected More..
reviews.body: Purchased this to use on our boat to rinse off. I had high expectations for this and it did not meet them. Water pressure was laughable, the pump would take what seemed like forever to draw water to the spray nozzles. It was very easy to assemble and switch out the different nozzles and batteries (came with two) but no where near enough water pressure to really make an impact on anything.

reviews.title: Defective, Bad charging
reviews.body: I purchase this for camping.I charged both batteries the day before I left but one did not work at all and the other one last it very little.No enough time to shower.I don't recommend buying this product.

reviews.title: Didn’t last
reviews.body: Stopped working after first try 🥺

reviews.title: Did not work
reviews.body: This sprayer would not work, so it was returned

reviews.title: Does not work
reviews.body: I was so excited to use this for rinsing shower after I scrub it . Read reviews and they were all good - minus 1. Well, charged it tried it- nope won’t pump any water.

reviews.title: Not all parts were in the package when bought!
reviews.body: Too late to return but I’ll write the review anyway. I bought this for an emergency kit in case of a power outage in various emergencies. It was never opened. Well now we have an emergency. I opened it to set up and the recharge cord for the battery pack is not in the package. It’s not a cord that is easily replaced because the connector is specifically made for this device. So the entire unit is now dead in the water. (pun intended) Off to find a store that MIGHT have a portable camp style shower system. Grrrr*side note* OPEN all items ordered to assure that all parts are there when purchased! Lessen learned!

reviews.title: Unstable, may not work
reviews.body: for -3 stars : one out of 3 times the water don't come while pump is sounding full run. I guess the valve malfunctioning.for -1 star : They don't offer Kohl's for return

reviews.title: Both batteries died
reviews.body: It works but not for long. It's very hard to keep water from getting on the battery when its hanging on the shower head. So over a short period of time you will begin to have problems, where the pressure of the water slowly reduces over time until it either stops working or the suction is so weak only drops of water fall. One of my batteries stopped working the other barely gets water through, so I bought a water proof one and I will see how that one goes, and its quite literally the same price. Just search it up on Amazon and its a black portable sprayer that shows you how much battery is left for about 39$ and rounding up to 41 using prime with the tax. This sprayer is 47$ with tax. So the other one is cheaper and you dont have to worry about the battery dying since its water proof

reviews.title: BIG DISAPPOINTMENT
reviews.body: I am so disappointed with this product.  I ordered them in May to have ready for a late June beach trip.  I purchased one as a gift for a friend joining us, and one for ourselves.  I have a set of the 1st generation and they have been awesome on our camping trips.  When I saw that I could use these with the 1st generation it was a no brainer, having extra battery packs available to rotate while recharging.  Prior to our trip I made sure all battery packs were charged.  When we got out to the beach, they did not work!  The water did not flow.  I charged the battery packs, again.  When I hooked it up to the pump, nothing.  I used my 1st generation battery pack with the new pump and it worked beautifully.  So it was not the pump, it's the battery packs.  Unfortunately I did not try them upon receiving them and I have missed the deadline to return the product.  It is disappointing that they do not work.  Big suggestion, as soon as you receive them, charge them and test drive them.  If they don't work, return them immediately.  Or just purchase a different brand.  I am disappointed since you could switch out the older units with the new ones.  But now I am stuck with non working battery packs.  I can't really give it a star.  The pump works, but what good is that if the battery packs do not work.

reviews.title: Junk!
reviews.body: The pump itself stopped working after a month.  The batteries were completely charged but the pump is junk.  I would give negative stars if allowed.

reviews.title: Avoid
reviews.body: Stopped working after 2 months of use :/

reviews.title: No charger included useless
reviews.body: Unit came with missing parts. Instructions indicate very clearly that item 11 is the charger and so the unit it’s useless for me if there is no charger.

reviews.title: About what you expect.
reviews.body: Does a good job, really.Not a fan of the battery/cable design. Have to hang it upside down or bend the power cord to the pump. If attached to water line, power button hangs down towards bucket.Did come with two batteries however. They last about 45 minutes each on a full charge and will pump the water with no problem. Take about an hour to recharge each.Good flow, until your kidlet drops it several times.Could use a better filter on the pump though. Picks up debris that might blow into bucket.Update...... Save your money. This lasted about 3 months then the batteries completely failed. It comes with two and both died. Will not take a charge at all.

reviews.title: Lightweight and compact solution, but I don't think its reliable enough to use on a regular basis.
reviews.body: Very lightweight and compact design. It also comes with a trigger nozzle which is helpful to turn on and off the water flow. Flow rate is good, not super powerful, but enough to rinse off a lather.  However, the pump stopped working after the 3rd time I used this product.  The other problem with the design is the fact that you have to keep the battery from getting wet.

reviews.title: Pump is underpowered - make sure you can hang your bucket/water supply above the shower head
reviews.body: Basically the pump does not have enough power to place it in a bucket on the ground and hold the shower head or the alternate sprayer above you. Or even a foot or two above the top of your bucket. Where I could hang the bucket up and use the shower head at the same or lower level, it gave a passable amount of water - but barely any better than the non-motorized models offered on Amazon for less. Would suggest considering one of those instead.

reviews.title: Hose comes off shower head  you lose water
reviews.body: The shower head is nice to save water, however the hose slides on and the pressure of the water blows it off and water pours out.  There is no way to attach a hose clamp because of the connection.  This is not worth the money, the battery lasts for 3 showers but runs out of water that pours out because it blows off.  Need to revamp the design.

reviews.title: revised review
reviews.body: The pump stopped working after about a dozen uses so have to change the review rating.  It did well when it worked but there is no explanation why it would just stop.

reviews.title: Worked great at first, but didn't last
reviews.body: Bought this at the first of July to use for doing the dishes while camping. Tested it when it arrived and (after putting the O ring in correctly) it worked incredibly well. Love the kitchen sprayer attachment. Bought a large water container that the pump fit down in, placed batteries, sprayers, and pump all in its own bag and stored it all in the camper for our next trip.Unfortunately when we finally got to go camping at the first of September, the unit failed to work at all. I assumed the batteries had lost their charge, but after charging again until the light turned blue, the unit still would not turn on after clicking the button. Neither battery made a difference. After so many positive reviews, I assume this was just a dud but I don't want to risk another $50 to find out.

reviews.title: Poor battery life
reviews.body: I would have given 5 stars if both batteries hadn't stopped working within 5 months of buying.

reviews.title: Check batteries before using
reviews.body: Not the best user friendly unit but functions.  One of the batteries will not charge

reviews.title: It's a leaker
reviews.body: It leaks at the hose connections. It took over 30 minutes to obtain pressure enough to have a stream of water coming from the showerhead.

reviews.title: very dissapointing
reviews.body: It worked fine the first two times we used it.  Then it would only come on sometimes, or you'd have to push the on/off button several times to get it to work.  After about 20 uses, the cheap plastic handle on the nozzle came off and then it completely stopped working.  A total waste of money.  The only reason I gave it two stars is because it had good water pressure when it did work.

reviews.title: Stopped working after 3 uses. Wish I could return.
reviews.body: I bought this to give my large rescue dog a bath outside since the bathtub became an impossible feat. The first 3 times I used it, it solved most of our issues with the dreaded bath time. However despite me storing the batteries fully charged they both stopped working after only 3 uses. Now I know why the return window of time is so brief. Such a shame because it was quite handy.

reviews.title: Sometimes pump takes a timeout in the middle of a warm shower.
reviews.body: We went on a three-week van trip and were very excited about this shower kit. When it squirted water, everything was great but even with a full battery and a pot full of water, once in a while nothing came out for while, leaving you standing there cold, full of soap and wondering what to do. It eventually worked on & off again but we haven't used it again. I'm not sure if we received a glitchy unit or if these pumps take time out on all units?

reviews.title: Get What You Pay For
reviews.body: The first one the pump died after one test. Just a 10 second test. Second one the shower head leaks at the connectors. Let's see what it's like after one use.

reviews.title: Proprietary charging cable - steer clear of this garbage.
reviews.body: Has a proprietary charging cable rendering the entire unit worthless if you lose it. Just use the accepted Industry standard instead of this garbage that doesn't work.  Do not buy this

reviews.title: Pump quit working after a month
reviews.body: I used it for about a month and then the pump would no longer work.

reviews.title: Just got around to trying it out but disappointed
reviews.body: Full disclosure I bought this product March 30th 2021. I did not get around to using it or trying it out or anything until today because I don't have any vacation time to be able to go camping so I was waiting until this next summer to go on my trip and I decided to check out the shower while I had a few minutes since I'm on Christmas holiday or whatever you call it the six inches of snow outside I plugged it in and let it charge. I attached it to the pump put it in a large jar of water the pump not the battery....it ran for literally 3 minutes then died....Not so impressed... if i had been out on my adventures I would have been stinky from lack of shower....

reviews.title: It stopped working.
reviews.body: I bought this product for camping and used it to occasionally wash my car outside and water my plants in the summer.  It wasn't always reliable.  It stopped working after approximately one and a half short seasons.  I loved it when it was working and it was too much money to spend on something that didn't last long enough.  I'm going to try a Nemo Helio and I'm hopeful for a better quality product that lasts several years.  I would appreciate a amazon refund

reviews.title: Poor Durability
reviews.body: We bought this to shower down the dogs several times a week after each visit to the local beach. It replaced the short-lived RinseKit portable shower system, and it has been a big improvement with superior water pressure and spray duration. Unfortunately, the parts are of poor quality. One of the sprayers failed at 6 months, and one of the batteries stopped working (5.5mm connector failed) at 8 months. It's priced $14 less now, but I would have been willing to pay $50 additional for better durability and a longer product life. This is yet another disposable product that will add to our landfill after less than a year's use. It will now be replaced by a real electric pump powered via a car cigarette charger.

reviews.title: Stopped working after 2 months
reviews.body: I loved this product when it worked. Two months later and only 4 uses the unit has stopped working. Batteries are lighting up but the unit just clicks and dose nothing. Very devastating.

reviews.title: Stopped working after 1 use
reviews.body: We used it on a camping trip in 2022. The trip was 3 days. We put it away for the season and this year, 2023, it will not charge. Very disappointing.

reviews.title: Bottomline: the battery requires too much charge babysitting to be useful.
reviews.body: So the actual pump and system works great when it is working. The problem is with the battery.  They do not maintain a charge in storage. This is in the instruction manual.  This means you must put it out and charge them every 2 months, and not doing that can damage the batteries. If you only use it a few times a year camping or as a backup for power outages you must pull it out and charge it regularly. A better solution would be to use better batteries or give us an option to use over-the-counter batteries.For me, that meant  I was dying my hair and when I went to wash out the dye the on-demand water heater broke so I pulled out this camp shower.I heated water and got a bucket and tried to use it -it failed me right when I needed it. The batteries needed to be charged because it had been in storage for over 6 months without use. Also one battery never charged at all.Bottomline: the battery requires too much charge babysitting.

reviews.title: Only shower nozzle works now
reviews.body: Used the dishwashing nozzle twice and not again - stopped working.  Contacted seller to see if I could buy a replacement nozzle but they refused.  Now can only use the shower nozzle, which has no on/off option.  Wastes water that way.

reviews.title: Good Concept-Poor Execution
reviews.body: The idea is great here, a portable shower/sprayer that is lightweight and runs off rechargeable batteries.  What doesn't work is the angle that the motor has to sit in a water bucket.  If it's not kept upright, the flow of water stops.  Since it's bottom heavy, it'll fall down sideways.  If all the air bubbles are not out, the pump doesn't work.  The hose is too short.  It's real finicky and not worth the effort and fuss.

reviews.title: Great till it stopped working
reviews.body: Bought this four months ago for my grooming business. Now neither battery will hold charge or work. I used the charger it came with and was careful not to wet the batteries. They don’t sell replacement batteries to my knowledge so $45 down the toilet and I have to spend another $50 getting a new one (somewhere else)

reviews.title: Works great till it wont
reviews.body: Worked well for around 3 months but then water pump died as it doesnt seem to even get power when connected to the charger/battery

reviews.title: Stopped working after 1 use
reviews.body: Only worked the first time I used it. Unable to get it to work again. Worked awesome when it did

reviews.title: Won't last long.
reviews.body: (review after usage) Worked okay for 6 months, then stopped working. The water wouldn't pump out. Batteries are good and still functioning but of no use.

reviews.title: Worked great until it didn't
reviews.body: This was a five-star review until just recently won the unit stopped working reliably. If I wiggle the cable just right I can get it to work for a moment but it no longer just turns on and works the way it used to. Seems to be an issue with the connection between the battery and or the pump rather than a malfunction of the pump itself.Prior to that, I love it. Inexpensive, the pump fits through a narrow mouth so I can use it with a lot of jugs and it worked really well. I had no complaints and all praise. I just wish it still worked.

reviews.title: battery cable too short
reviews.body: This unit has one short coming and that is the cable to the battery is too short. I don't understand why they had to make it so short and awkward to use. The pressure and volume of water and the battery run time and charge time are okay but why not make the cable as long as the rest of the unit. This is just silly and for that only 3 stars.

reviews.title: Not sure if I would use it again
reviews.body: Its an okay shower. If the hose us fullystreched out , the pump has a hard time sending the water up so the pressure quality deteriorates. I would recommend elevating your water container as close to the shower head as possible for better performance.

reviews.title: Worked great, but ...
reviews.body: Worked great, but second battery stopped working and now I’m  down to 1 battery.  My main issue is finding a water storage container with a large enough opening for the pump part, but  which seals well and doesn’t cause water to slosh all over my car/truck when driving

reviews.title: Battery concern - excellent pump
reviews.body: Like the product but both batteries died three months later. The real issue is that there is no battery replacement available.

reviews.title: Works good but battery doesn't last long
reviews.body: The pump and shower head work good but I wish they still sold the older style that just took regular or rechargeable d batteries. These batteries are lithium rechargeable. I got two and you should also because they do not hold charge long and you will need both batteries. Recharging them also takes a while.

reviews.title: Works as advertised!
reviews.body: Overall it works as advertised and does what I need it to do.  I only use the sprayer for my needs and the pressure is okay, could be stronger.  But giving it only 3 stars because one of the batteries stopped working after just 2 uses.  Will have to see if the other one lasts and will update review if it doesn't.

reviews.title: good pump bad battery
reviews.body: the pump is good but the battery isn’t a lasting product

reviews.title: Leaking at the hose/shower head connection
reviews.body: There is a substantial leak at the location where the hose line meets the shower head connection. I will return if seller doesn’t contact to fix.

reviews.title: Decent pump
reviews.body: I have been using this for 10 - 15 minutes daily for 4 months in an off grid experiment. It works very well for taking a shower or rinsing things off. The spray and volume of water are good. Only problem is recently the pump will shut off during use. I believe it is a problem with the battery switch. All in all its good for intermittent use but ymmv on longevity. I would buy it again if I couldn't find a better alternative.Update: after about 5 months the pump gave up. I haven't tried to take it apart yet to determine the point of failure. I really liked this thing and would consider replacing it with like unit if it had lasted longer or was less expensive. I'm up in the air about recommending it. I originally thought the problem was a battery but both batteries do the same thing and I think it unlikely both are bad.

reviews.title: cheap plastic but works
reviews.body: It works okay with enough water pressure. But the battery needs to be recharged often and it takes a long time to recharge them. The plastic housing is cheap, I suspect these will not last long. It meets my need to the most part. Looking at the design of this product, I think I should be able to put together a similar shower by assembling a water pump, and a battery pack.

reviews.title: It worked
reviews.body: This worked as expected. I'm using it to wash dishes the only thing is when you turn on the pump it does start to leak water out of the nozzle from the pressure. For use and pressure it worked good, and the batteries lasted through 10 gallons of water before having to charge. It took about 2 hours to charge off the generator power.

reviews.title: It’s ok
reviews.body: Came with 2 rechargeable batteries and one is them will not work or charge. Came with 2 different shower heads the traditional one is low pressure but sucks up water like crazy, the sprayer saves on water cuz u can drop the water and it has better pressure, not enough pressure to wash a car or atv like it states. Maybe good for plants or Camping dishes or light rinses.

reviews.title: 1 battery won't charge
reviews.body: I'm not sure if the pump is quite strong enough. I know that one of the batteries won't charge.

reviews.title: Over priced for what you get
reviews.body: First off let me say I bought this during hot water line repair at home.Was able to shower with it so it should function for a camping shower. Also used it to wash dogs during repairs at the house. This was actually more usefull than the shower. Biggest issue with shower is the head provided does not have a valve to turn it off, however if it did the pump will cavitate and not resume flow.  So you have to turn it off and on during shower process.Not a good value for the price paid but it is functional.

reviews.title: It's okay. Not worth the price.
reviews.body: We bought this one specifically because it was a battery operated pump system, not a valve and gravity system. We were going to be camping in the desert and couldn't depend on there being trees to hang one of those other shower bags from, so I bought this. I tested it at home, the batteries took a long time to charge but they did charge, but the pump is pretty low power. And because of how it's designed it won't pick up the last inch or so of water, which obviously is a problem. Also, there's a warning saying you can't submerge the batteries, but if you're showering in an enclosed space I'm not sure how you'd keep them dry unless you put a ziplock bag around them. I will say the pressure is okay, but not great. Overall it's a pretty poorly designed product and most definitely not worth $50. Returned it.

reviews.title: Non working battery
reviews.body: The kids shower broke so until we fix it we got this camping shower....After about a few uses one of the batteries stopped working. Kind of upsetting.

reviews.title: Works great, when it works.
reviews.body: Have used this for showering for the past 1.5 years. It works great, most of the time. After about 2 months the cord that attaches to the battery pack stopped working unless it’s bent over 180 degrees. Must be some kind of short in the wire. I haven’t been able to find anything that mentions a warranty. Would love to give this 5 stars. It really works well when it works but when it doesn’t, it’s a cold reminder that I should have bought a different unit. If there was product support I’d love to know about it.

reviews.title: Have a problem in one battery pack
reviews.body: I charged both battery packs and have a problem with one of them. For some reason it will not stay on. I rechecked the cord connection and I hold the on button several seconds after it starts running but it will not continue to operate when I remove my finger.The battery works well.How can I get some advice or a replacement?

reviews.title: ok buy, 3 and a bit stars
reviews.body: Nice but has issues. It came with 2 separate battery packs. I had expected this one to have less oomph, but it has good pressure. I've accidentally pulled the pump out of the water for a few seconds, luckily it still worked, but it did need priming to continue my shower, To prime it I had to shake it about underwater while pulsing the motor on and off. Until I did that, I thought I'd burnt it out.These detachable batteries are convenient. On the other hand the shower head attachment is terrible and the head can simply fall off the pipe.Oh, and while plugged into a charger the batteries have an incredibly bright blue light. Don't charge where you're trying to sleep!I haven't tried the trigger shower attachment.We're using the shower as a bathroom shower and as long as the pipe remains jammed into the shower head it gives us a better shower than the mains unit.

reviews.title: Not worth the price
reviews.body: So it does sort of work. Only one battery actually works. The other battery will pump the water out ona full charge but it takes a while to get it going.

reviews.title: I had higher hopes
reviews.body: I actually rate this a 2.5 as I had higher hopes for this product. I'm currently staying somewhere off-grid that only runs super hot water and so I thought this could be a good solution for me for taking showers without scalding myself. I will say this, it is pretty easy to use and intuitive. Keep in mind that you should make sure you have enough water in your bucket/water holder before you begin your shower. I wish this had an on/off on the actual shower head or water pump as it's awkward the way it is now. The suction cup and shower head holder are really frustrating to use and seems an afterthought. Overall the plastic used in this product is like what you'd find for a child's toy and there are some design misses that make using it a bit frustration. Too bad, this could have been a great solution.  I do think it works well as a "trial run" for those considering how to shower while camping--it made me think I probably want to get one of these portable butane powered showers that run $300-400 when I have the money as that's really what I need and this little guy doesn't cut it for me. best of luck!

reviews.title: Overall good, but dispenser end leaks like crazy
reviews.body: Battery and pump are great with good suction.  When I connected the dispenser end, it leaked like crazy which meant I had to constantly turn it on and off.  Consider buying a replacement adaptor to ensure it seals properly.

reviews.title: Water Hose won't stay on pump
reviews.body: I have  trouble keeping the hose attached to the pump. As soon as I turned it on, the hose would come off the pump squirting water every where. It needs a clamp or something..

reviews.title: Stopped working...
reviews.body: I really liked the product, used it once almost everyday for 3 to 5 minutes and then put it away until the next day. Worked great for about 2 months. Now it's deader than a hammer...

reviews.title: Returned it
reviews.body: Te previous version is better. This one had problems. Didn't pump the water, jus6made the noise and the switch is not practical.  Better the other design wit the switch on the unit

reviews.title: Great Shower for Off-grid
reviews.body: Purchased this is Jan, it worked great!  We had a shower, with running water in the exact temperature we wanted. We live off grid without running water so this was a great addition for our camp that we live in year round. In July one of the batteries stopped working. The on off button wouldn’t work anymore. Wouldn’t charge. We used the other one until the same thing happened. It seems like something different could perhaps make this shower greater. The on off button could be different. And separate from the battery maybe. I kept the battery dry, even though it had to be close to the shower and running water, but it seemed like normal wear and tear from usage is what caused it to stop working. I see that there is a warranty on the battery but I can’t find the place to replace it! I’d love to order a new one, and I still have the old one except my bf took it apart to make another shower from his boat pump and a boat battery. It works ok but the water pressure is not like this shower. I loved that I could switch shower head to the sprayer and the pump fit right into our seven gallon water holder. This shower is great and we used it everyday and I’m not happy with the replacement we got.

reviews.title: Works great but battery life is not great
reviews.body: The products works great . Just needs to improve its battery life. Make sure you don't let the water goes low otherwise air would get sucked by the motor . My only complaint is its battery. It could be improved. This product could be devolved and make way more sales .

reviews.title: Work great if the water source is close & once you get it to turn on
reviews.body: I used the shower to wash dishes while camping. Did great with hot water but it always took a few tries to get the pump to turn on. Once on I had to adjust the location of the water source to work.

reviews.title: Mixed bag
reviews.body: This is okay but fussing with the rechargeable batteries is a pain and I found the water pressure was relatively light. Moreover, battery life was poor. Though many would dismiss country of origin criticism and it was not my primary concern, origin of manufacture is Peoples' Republic of China if that matters to you. All of that said, if I had opportunity to purchase again, in candor but with respect to manufacturer and seller, I would use a cup dipped in a bucket to toss water at myself to acheive better results.

reviews.title: Worth giving it a try  !
reviews.body: Got this in April and the first battery died in June , after like maybe 3-4 uses , the charging light will stay on forever and battery didn t charge at all . Contacted the seller , they promised they will send me two replacement batteries in 30-35 days ( coming from China ) . It s August the second battery just died too couple of days ago , it charges but the on/off button isn t working anymore . I m still waiting for replacements .When it was working it does its job , you can shower with it without problems there s not much more that you would need . Just hang it somewhere start it and shower away.Later edit :The batteries were sent ( late but they were eventually sent , they are on the way ) and the seller shipped a new unit free of cost to replace the faulty one . Hopefully this one will be problems free , customer service is doing a good  job !

reviews.title: Good portable/emergency shower
reviews.body: We live in a building where the hot water goes out 2-3 times a year. We bought this for such occasions.  We had to use it once so far. The pressure and water flow are good, and works great paired with a 5 gallon bucket. The only problem we had is one of the batteries does not work, but the seller is sending me a new battery

reviews.title: Great product
reviews.body: The dish sprayer is great. The showerhead also works well and has good pressure, but there is no way to shut it off, so even after the pump is off, it can still maintain a siphon and spray water out. Maybe this won't happen if it's hanging up. I had the pump in a pot on the counter and the head next to it in the sink just testing how it worked. Overall I'd say it's a good product and handy to have around for camping, or power outages as long as you charge the battery ahead of time.

reviews.title: Love it, mostly
reviews.body: Really good water flow for a portable.  Estimate 1 gallon a minute.  On/off button on battery ok placement but I keep the battery sealed on a ziplock since I have to touch it with a wet hand to turn it on and off. Wish immersible pump cord that runs to the battery was a bit longer.  Very happy with the flow and for the size it’s pretty incredible.

reviews.title: Great camp shower with multiple uses and 2 battery packs two thumbs up.
reviews.body: I bought this portable camp shower as an emergency if we lost power and have an easier way to take a shower for myself and for my kids. It turns out I was also able to use it for other things such as helping my husband water his garden. I've been using this camp shower for more than two weeks now so I have gained insight as to how strong the water pressure was, tips and tricks and all . first off I was little wary of the price cuz it is a bit high. However after looking at all the other options this camp shower looked to be the best of all so I sucked it up and bought it. They provide you with two batteries which is great. The fact that the battery pack and water vacuum part is separated is very beneficial. I don't have to worry about the battery pack getting wet as I can just stick it in my pocket. The next thing I like about this is that it works well for my purposes which is to shower and helping to water the garden. I did test the shower head just to see how strong it was and the water pressure was not that strong as a normal shower obviously but it was alright for emergencies. Now the other shower head had much stronger water pressure and this is what I used to help water to Garden. Frankly, if you wanted a stronger water pressure for showering this other shower head might be the one you would prefer to use. I used a stronger water pressure shower head for gardening because I wanted the ability for the water to travel far as we had several garden beds. The second shower head accomplished that as the water pressure was stronger than the first shower head. here are some dislike or trouble I had but these were pretty minor to me. In the beginning I could not figure out why the water would not come out sometimes when I dropped the water pump thing into the bucket and turned it on It would sputter and I had to turn it on and off and then it would work. It turns out there's a trick to this. When you drop the water pump vacuum thingy into the bucket you have to press the shower head and don't release you're hold on it as you turn the shower on. Other wish list I would have liked what does that mean for the hoses and cords to be longer. It would also have been nice if the charging cable was a USB. But other than this minor pet peeves I still really like this camp shower and if it lasts for a long time even better and it would justify the $50 price. I will update this review if anything else occurs but so far I am very happy with discount shower.

reviews.title: Next Level Camping
reviews.body: Having certain amenities of home at the camp site really makes the whole experience of camping that much more enjoyable. This battery powered pump is definitely one of those welcomed amenities! All in all this is a good product. I do HIGHLY recommend breaking in the battery before your first excursion into the wilderness. What do I mean? Charge the batteries out of the box (red light will turn blue) and then run the batteries all the way down. Then charge them again. I was fortunate enough to be car camping with an inverter, so I was able to recharge the batteries, but the first battery runtime DID NOT last nearly as long as the following charges. I also recommend heating up water on a stove before washing dishes, to help dissolve the grease on your dishes. Some complaints: I wish this pump had a built in pressure gauge that would automatically shut off the pump when the water wasn't being used. Most RV water pumps have this feature and would be welcomed on this product to save battery life. 2. I wish there was an option to run the pump off of a car's cigarette lighter, instead of just the portable batteries. 3. The included clips are clumsy and not well designed. I wish they was more thought put into them, so the faucet sprayer was able to be securely mounted to the side of a plastic storage container or a 5 gallon utility pale. Other than these complaints, this is a worthy purchase at 50 bucks. Remember to drain and recharge the batteries at least once every 3 months or the batteries will dry out! Nothing new

reviews.title: Great camping equipment
reviews.body: Having 2 batteries is great .they last ñong enough to get the job done .the water once the pump is primed goes high abo e the water source .Its a ñittle tricky ti prime but not a big deal

reviews.title: Works Great as a Camping Shower
reviews.body: Water pressure was adequate, though not strong. I liked using the spray nozzle better than the shower head because the water flow stops when you release the lever, which allows you to conserve water during your shower.Minor negatives: Air locks can happen but are easy to fix by draining the water from the pump and starting again. It comes with 2 batteries, which is great, but keeping track of which one is fully charged is a challenge! I use a piece of masking tape with "charged" written on it, to let me know that I should be using the other one until it quits.

reviews.title: Seems good out of the box.
reviews.body: I have not put this to the test during a camping trip. However, I will say that testing it in our sink, this unit has very good pressure considering it’s smaller size. I am reading this four stars for now. Once I put this to use in a real world situation I will come back and provide more information and update my rating. 5/31/2021

reviews.title: Was 2 Stars but now 4.
reviews.body: Updated Review: the priming issue that was in my original review must have been a temporary fluke.  For the last year we have used this shower pump many times and it has served us well.  Good preasure and flow and batteries are still holding up well.Original Review: I received the pump in a timely manner and the packaging / pump were in good order.  Charged batteries and assembled the unit and tested it.  Test went well and I got the pressure I was expecting.  Having used it a few times in conjuncture with some water Jerry cans it generally works ok.  The only complaint, and is significant, is that when the pump looses prime it can have a really hard time getting it back.  I removed the sediment filter and that seemed to improve the issue a little bit.  I am pumping clean water so I'm not worried about anything getting in the pump.  I have put the pump away twice with it not accomplishing it's task.  I now use it as a back up just incase.Battery life is good and pressure it decent for what it is.

reviews.title: Pleasantly surprised, but make sure you purge the air by giving it a little shake first.
reviews.body: We love to go camping but my wife absolutely must have a shower available to her or at least the ability to wash her hair.  So we gave this shower a try and for the money it can't be beat!  We took a 5-Gallon bucket, but she only used about 2.5-3 Gallons to shampoo and condition her hair altogether.  We didn't use the big round shower head, just the little trigger-head which I think gives a little bit more control.I think a lot of people get frustrated because at times it doesn't seem to pump... it's important that you have to find a way to "prime" the pump to get water to get into the pump itself before it'll start to push water, if not then it's just spinning in air and not getting anything to move.To prime the pump, once it's submerged in water, turn it upside down and give it a shake to get the air bubbles out... then when you turn it on, you can hear the tone change and you should have water.  If not, then while hitting the trigger use something... your mouth... whatever to suck water through for a moment and you'll get water flowing.  It's just fluid-dynamics and this is a cheap pump... you can't expect it to be self-priming.Either way, did the job great and my wife definitely feels more comfortable doing a longer trip.

reviews.title: It is what it is over priced bilge pump
reviews.body: It is what it is!Over priced bilge pump

reviews.title: Es muy util
reviews.body: Lo puedo llevar a todos lados y facil de usar solo fuera mejor con mas fuerza el motor

reviews.title: Seems to be of quality
reviews.body: The materials- while plastic seem sturdy and well constructed.  The battery was this powerful ( see video) after about an hour charge.  Nice pressure.  We will charge both batteries overnight before we take off camping.  I saw one review about needing to blow thru the hose if the draw gets sluggish due to air pockets and that did work when I switched from the shower sprayer to the pressure wash nozzle and had inadvertently lifted the pump out of the water.  After I blew from the nozzle end of the tube ( nozzle off, of course) into the water bucket, the pump worked perfectly again.  This, and a camp shower / potty tent were the last things we needed and now we have everything we need to be at home out in the woods.  I should point out that the shower nozzle just starts flowing immediately upon starting the battery whereas the sink-style pressure nozzle only flows with the lever pressed, same as your sink sprayer at home.  After we actually use it camping, i'm hoping to come back and 5 star this rating.  We may need a second one to keep in the camp kitchen, although this is very easily portable enough to use at both places.  I'll find a small plastic covered tote bin to store the sprayer kit with hose, nozzles, batteries, pump, and hanging hardware or maybe just a 5 gallon bucket which would also hold hygiene products for the "bathroom" tent  There are spare o- rings too.

reviews.title: Efficient have had no issues in the past year
reviews.body: Bought for camping and had to use in my house due to the water heater dying on me.  Had no issues with the unit if you use the shower head it will empty a 5 gal bucket in under 5min.  no problems with batteries or charging.  Love the product will buy again.

reviews.title: Worked great creating a camp sink. But could only get one battery to work. Thus the four stars.
reviews.body: Our campsite spigot was a couple sites away so we had to haul water. So we created a camp sink. We use a 5 gallon bucket. A hole was cut in the center of the lid and dropped the pump in and the hose through the hole. The spay nozzle sat on the table along with a couple of collapsible sinks. When you needed water you just pushed the button on the battery pack to get the pump going and we had running water. When you were done, push the button to turn off the pump. One battery lasted a couple of days.  The only complaint I have is that one of the battery packs would not function. We charged it before leaving and it would not work. It charged but did not function. The light was on continuously and would not turn off. Contacted Amazon and they are sending out a replacement. Hope both work in the next one. Otherwise, great addition to our camping gear.

reviews.title: I like it BUT...
reviews.body: This darn thing has a tendency of not working when I squeeze the lever. Pull the hose from the pump, pump works fine. Check the hose, no issue. Hook it back up and it may give me a drizzle or nothing. Mess with it some more, and it works fine. I cannot tell how irritating it is when ur ready to bathe and then delayed dealing with a fickle thing. I've never had it not work, but 50% of the time I have to mess with it.Once it wants to cooperate with me, it works exactly like it should. Happy, but wish it cooperated more without having to mess it.

reviews.title: My review/take on this
reviews.body: EDIT/ADD-ON: So I am still using this thing and it didn't shut off on me, but it stopped pumping, I could hear the little engine going, but no water pumping. I was almost done with my shower and just needed to rinse off. I read on a very HELPFUL review that same thing happened and it might have been the temperature of the engine/water. between 50F to 122F is able to work. I did not have it it higher then 122F because that is way to hot for me. How ever,,,,while I was getting the water ready, I was pouring into the buck with the pump in it with hot water, keep in mind it is a 5 gallon bucket with 3 gallons of cold water first. It might have just over heated via usage. I did try to take it apart thinking it was clogged, but I couldn't figure out how to take it apart without damaging it, it seemed. So I saw that review and just now tried it in some cold water and it works. Will update it need.Ok, so far so good. Got to use it today. Suction power awesome, battery life, I guess great for now. I did make sure to charge one battery fully before testing anything out. I do have concerns though. The battery has a label stating that it is not water proof at all. Which is weird because it is hard to turn off during the use. The button isn't that hard to press but you have to have a grip on it a bit to hold down the button to turn on and to turn off. Wish there was another way. I don't like the fact the port and the charger isn't some style of USB C type or micro USB. Maybe future models will. Thinking this just in case the charger gets lost and or the batter is Unreliable and or un-replaceable, then you have a worthless thing because you can't get power. Again with the power switch, wish it was easier to turn on and off. I did how ever have two 5 gallon buckets ready to test/use and made it easy to take a full shower without running out of warm water to fast. All so since the spray hose nozzle is an on off, but the shower head doesn't have that, will it hurt the motor of the suction engine?

reviews.title: Works really well
reviews.body: I would have gave this a five star rating. But when I went to charge both batteries only one worked. A side from that a good unit.

reviews.title: Product works well!
reviews.body: So far, the product works as advertised, but what I really like about it is it has two different nozzles for the hose. The spray nozzle works better than expected, has quite a bit of pressure when using. Haven’t put the batteries to the full test yet but it’s nice that it comes with two. The diameter of the pump itself is larger than 2 inches so it may not fit inside of some jugs or barrels. So far very happy with the product. This will be used mostly at our off-grid cabin and the convenience of pressurized water in a simple form is an added bonus.

reviews.title: Does what I need it to do.
reviews.body: It has no on/off switch but there is an on/off on the battery that it runs off of, so that is how I shut it off and on while using it. It's a bit of a pain and I'm afraid I'll wear out the button over repeated use but only time will tell. I wish the pump would run until it reaches a certain pressure and then turn off so it would be unnecessary to do that .The shower head will drain by gravity if lower than or equal to the level of the pump, even when battery is turned off but the dish sprayer will not. So I used the dish sprayer for my last shower and it worked nice. Both dish sprayer and shower head go through a ton of water so even if you shut it off quickly, you're going to need a lot of water in your bucket.So I wish they had considered the situation people are in more carefully and made the heads much more water efficient. I bought a different shower head to use on it and hope that it will make the difference.

reviews.title: Water pump stop working and now it works
reviews.body: Update: Recently after another try, it finally worked. The pump was out of water for too long. Before using it, the pump needs to submerge into water and I shaked it to put all the air out. It is working now.Original I would give this pump 4 stars on April 2023 when I purchased this. It was working fine then, but the pump stop working for me today. I have only used it few times on April this year. I can't believe its life is so short. So disappointed!

reviews.title: Update:Doesn't always pump and no customer service or website.
reviews.body: When first testing I had to turn the pump off & on 5 times before it finally pumped water. I tried to shower but had to turn off/on 7 times. There's no website or direct way to contact seller so I had to go through Amazon. Their response did not even address the problem so I have to go through Amazon again. When it worked it was fine.If you suck the water through the hose before attaching the accessories it works fine. Without it the air blocks the water. They could have told me that.

reviews.title: Works well in both shower and spray modes
reviews.body: Bought this for quick showers when off grid camping. I use a 5 gallon bucket and this will easily pull the water from ground level to the top of the hose and still have decent pressure. This has 2 nozzle types one is a standard shower head, the other is a trigger operated spray head.  The shower head doesn't have an off function like the spray head does I think that would be a real nice feature so I find on the shower head it kind of wastes water. Now this can be recharged with a USB power bank or even a small solar panel. I was easily able to take 2 full showers with one battery and it said there was still 3/4 power left and it also comes with 2 batteries. Now about the batteries. They use a proprietary connection so you have to use the special USB charging cable they give you to charge them......there is only 1 cable provided so don't lose or break it or . I have not been able to figure out if there is any way to get a second or replacement charging cable. Also the batteries are not waterproof but I would say if you splash them by accident; and yes I have done that they should be fine, just don't drop them into the water bucket. So 4 stars due to the special charging cable that is needed and that they only provide one of those cables.

reviews.title: Great off grid shower!
reviews.body: I bought this about a year ago and have used it a few times, for off grid camping and washing the dog.  I made one little change...I bought a residential shower head with an on/off button.  Fortunately those are universal and it fit on the Kedsun hose.  Now I can turn off the water without pressing the button on the battery.  Love that the battery is separate from the pump and isn't submerged.  Decent water pressure, very quiet, and has held up for a year.  I use less than a gallon of water to shower with the ability to turn off the water at the shower head, closer to two gallons if I'm washing my hair.  Don't know how long it will last but I will definitely buy again if my current one dies.

reviews.title: Keeping dishes sanitize
reviews.body: This little pump has worked excellent. I use it to rinse off dishes with chlorinated water to. It came with two head and the small sprayer head is great for water on demand.  This is my sink setup after hurricane damage and loss of flowing water.

reviews.title: Shower On The Go
reviews.body: Excellent shower kit!The battery powered motor provides a steady and powerful water flow.  Even though the majority of the product is plastic, it has proven to be durable and a decent value.  The motor is quiet and not intrusive.The size of the kit is perfect for travel and easy to set up and use.The only negative is that the batteries have quit charging after a few months with previous purchases and the batteries are not available for single purchase.Other than that, a good product.

reviews.title: Great Little Portable Shower
reviews.body: My old solar bag shower finally gave out and I purchased this instead.  For camping, or rinsing off dishes or at the beach, this is portable, lightweight and perfect.  The remote battery and switch make this easy to turn on and off.  It's also nice that you don't have to lift a heavy container of water over your head.For a warm shower, just heat the water ahead of time on a camp stove and add that to your container.  The drop-in pump diameter is small enough to fit inside most wide-mouth water containers, so you don't have to place it in an open bucket.The only downside to this unit is the electrical cord and hose are on the short side.  If you want the nozzle over your head, and you're 6 feet or taller, this is a problem.  The alternative is to place your water container on something like a camp chair or stool, or you can somehow get a longer hose and extension wire for this.The pump produces a pretty good stream of water, so it will exhaust your supply quickly, if you simply let it run.  But overall, it's a great product.

reviews.title: Surprising alternative
reviews.body: I bought this to double up on a like item that I could not find from the original manufacturer. This is exactly like the previous one. The unit WORKS just like the other.  The brochure information says it puts out 0.9 gallons an hour flow rate. I believe it does better. The pressure is excellent,  nearly as strong as domestic water supply. I have used it to assist with a temporarily handicapped family member who can not bathe or shower as usual. The portability and water pressure make daily routine easy.

reviews.title: WONDERFUL FOR WATERING THE GARDEN👍👍👍
reviews.body: This lightweight sprayer is the best thing that I’ve purchased in a long time.  I collect rain water and have to haul it to the garden up hill no less. I use my car so it’s easier for me.  Before I got this sprayer, I had to fill a watering can over and over and over, taking at least 2 to 3 hours to water my garden and other areas with flowers etc.  Now all I have to do is fill up 5gal buckets that are strategically placed throughout the garden areas, drop in the pump, put the battery pack in my pocket and water away.  This sprayer has reduced my watering time by 50% as well as water wastage. I put a fine filter sock around the pump for debris, ensuring that the pump keeps free of large and smaller particles. EXCELLENT OVER ALL PRODUCT, HIGHLY RECOMMEND. The batter life with constant use is an hour plus, also the fact that the batter IS NOT LOCATED ON THE SUBMERSIBLE PUMP makes it another valued bonus as you can put the battery in your pocket ensuring that it stays out of the water ensuring longer battery life..  It takes about 1 1/2 hours to fully charge the battery AND it comes with 2 batteries which is another fabulous bonus.  The hose is about 6’ long and is very easy to put on the pump.  Watering is a pleasure now instead of a timely chore. Excellent value and an excellent price👍👍👍👍👍👍

reviews.title: Easy to use
reviews.body: Took this for camping recently and it was a hit. It came with two of the power button to turn on the pump, but only needed one (aka one charge)! At least 10 people used this to take a shower at camp, we used a 5 gallon bucket to pump the water and it made life so easy. Recommend! 100%!

reviews.title: This shower system works well for my needs.
reviews.body: This is so easy to use. Fill a bucket with water, prime the pump and shower away. It doesn't require a lot of water to function.

reviews.title: Multi-purpose for camping
reviews.body: Works great as a camp shower or to wash feet off. Also found it very helpful to fill RV water tank at campgrounds without proper water spigots. The last place we went only had a hand pump for water, so I pumped into a 5 gallon jug then used the shower hose and pump to pump it straight into the inlet. Very happy with it so far.

reviews.title: So easy to use
reviews.body: Was extremely easy to assemble I overnight shipped it so I got it super fast you put the hose from the faucet head into the pump and then you put a battery onto the black cord the only drawback to this item is you have to have a wider mouth jug I ended up just pouring into a barrel that I had that I use in the canoe and that work for me but if this is one you want for a water bottle it may not work. This is actually my second time purchasing it my daughter loved it so much for her little ones that she carries it in the back of her truck when she goes to the beach to rinse the sand off

reviews.title: Great little shower, surprisingly powerful
reviews.body: This is an awesome little shower. We have used it for camping and it was a real life saver. I'm happy they included two batteries as I did have to change one out mid-shower once. The batteries last about 5 full showers with a 5 gallon bucket.  I don't recommend the shower head unless you have a lot of water as the little pump can pump out about a 5 gallon bucket in 2-3 minutes which is way too quick to get clean. We decided to use the sink attachment which works much better for controlling water flow with a limited amount of water.

reviews.title: Great Pump
reviews.body: Great pump, love the different ends.  First one wasn't charging so.they replaced it and this one is great so far

reviews.title: Super convenient
reviews.body: This is great for a camp without running water. It makes it so much easier for me to do my dishes. It’s wonderfully designed. It comes with two batteries so that makes it even better, but I want to point out that when I first submerged it into the water and turned it on. It didn’t wanna work so then I started it over again still didn’t work. It didn’t start pumping through until about the fifth restart so keep something like that in mind For when you’re doing yours if it has trouble starting out.  With that said, I’d recommend this to anybody that needs convenience of water.

reviews.title: Truck drivers staying clean
reviews.body: Otr Truck driver here, I bought this to be able to take showers for those days I'm stuck at shipper/receivers or at rest areas  this thing works great. I've had it for months and the pump still works great. My only issue is that the shower head doesn't have a shut off option, so I've only used the sink head.

reviews.title: Great quality!
reviews.body: This product is amazing!  Easy to use with great water pressure. Having two batteries is great since you can charge one while still using the pump.

reviews.title: Four people showered twice each over 2 days using one battery
reviews.body: We used this shower pump while camping on the New River in Southern Virginia. My sister made the enclosure from a light weight tarp and discarded tent poles. My brother-in-law is 6' 4" and there was plenty of length of the hose. The water pressure was excellent. I purposely bought this unit since the battery and on/off switch is attached to the pump by a suitably long cord. It worked better than we had hoped. It required about 5 gallons of water for 2 showers. We heated water on our camp stoves and added it to the 5 gallon bucket. We are VERY pleased with this shower and indeed may use it at places that have showers since we did not have to walk to a bathhouse.

groups.label: 泵体与耐久 · 长期使用稳定

quotes.snippet: Seems to be of quality：The materials- while plastic seem sturdy and well constructed. The battery was this powerful ( see video) aft…
…t pump：I have been using this for 10 - 15 minutes daily for 4 months in an off grid experiment. It works very well for taking a shower or rinsing things off. The spr…
Great device ... while it worked. WARNING! Major quality issues!：We used it several times and it was great ... while it lasted. However, the cable right at the plug t…
Poor Durability：We bought this to shower down the dogs several times a week after each visit to the local beach. It replaced…
…ome, which my family uses daily and they both arr 3 years old and are still working great.But recently I ordered a 3rd new one, and it never arrived even after a month…

reviews.title: Good idea but substandard product
reviews.body: Great idea but the quality is poor. It was a guessing game if the product (suction portion) would work or not with each power up. Glad to have attempted testing it out before a summer roadtrip. Returned the same day it was shipped after the many failed attempts and did not wish to be frustrated on an adventure.

reviews.title: Serious Battery issues!
reviews.body: BEWARE !! Many users have reported issue with the batteries ! Once battery started showing issues it’s not usable even for 10 seconds. Seller had promised for replacement batteries, but I never received them ! Finally, amazon came to rescue and approved the refund request ! Serious quality control issues with this product!

reviews.title: Poor Quality
reviews.body: Product failed after very little use and the company does not reply or want to replace it

reviews.title: Great device ... while it worked. WARNING! Major quality issues!
reviews.body: We used it several times and it was great ... while it lasted. However, the cable right at the plug that connects to the battery has developed a false connection. When I moved the showerhead while bathing, sometimes it would cut off. this got worse and worse, and tonight I realized that it was due to a bad connection in the plug that connects to the battery. Now I can't get it to turn on.They will only pay for $20 of return postage, but since I am in Mexico that makes it prohibitively expensive. Great concept. Crappy application. Sadly, it looks like a waste of $60 for me. I cannot recommend sure to long-term quality issues.I'll try contacting Kedsum customer service to see if they make it right, will edit if they do.

reviews.title: Poor battery quality
reviews.body: Batterys not charging fully or holding the charge well (supposed to blink 4 times for full charge,  charged for many hours, never achieved that. Since use, charge has not gone higher than 2 blinks)

reviews.title: It stopped working.
reviews.body: I bought this product for camping and used it to occasionally wash my car outside and water my plants in the summer.  It wasn't always reliable.  It stopped working after approximately one and a half short seasons.  I loved it when it was working and it was too much money to spend on something that didn't last long enough.  I'm going to try a Nemo Helio and I'm hopeful for a better quality product that lasts several years.  I would appreciate a amazon refund

reviews.title: Poor Durability
reviews.body: We bought this to shower down the dogs several times a week after each visit to the local beach. It replaced the short-lived RinseKit portable shower system, and it has been a big improvement with superior water pressure and spray duration. Unfortunately, the parts are of poor quality. One of the sprayers failed at 6 months, and one of the batteries stopped working (5.5mm connector failed) at 8 months. It's priced $14 less now, but I would have been willing to pay $50 additional for better durability and a longer product life. This is yet another disposable product that will add to our landfill after less than a year's use. It will now be replaced by a real electric pump powered via a car cigarette charger.

reviews.title: Worked great until it didn't
reviews.body: This was a five-star review until just recently won the unit stopped working reliably. If I wiggle the cable just right I can get it to work for a moment but it no longer just turns on and works the way it used to. Seems to be an issue with the connection between the battery and or the pump rather than a malfunction of the pump itself.Prior to that, I love it. Inexpensive, the pump fits through a narrow mouth so I can use it with a lot of jugs and it worked really well. I had no complaints and all praise. I just wish it still worked.

reviews.title: Not sure if I would use it again
reviews.body: Its an okay shower. If the hose us fullystreched out , the pump has a hard time sending the water up so the pressure quality deteriorates. I would recommend elevating your water container as close to the shower head as possible for better performance.

reviews.title: Decent pump
reviews.body: I have been using this for 10 - 15 minutes daily for 4 months in an off grid experiment. It works very well for taking a shower or rinsing things off. The spray and volume of water are good. Only problem is recently the pump will shut off during use. I believe it is a problem with the battery switch. All in all its good for intermittent use but ymmv on longevity. I would buy it again if I couldn't find a better alternative.Update: after about 5 months the pump gave up. I haven't tried to take it apart yet to determine the point of failure. I really liked this thing and would consider replacing it with like unit if it had lasted longer or was less expensive. I'm up in the air about recommending it. I originally thought the problem was a battery but both batteries do the same thing and I think it unlikely both are bad.

reviews.title: Perfect Product; Absolute Horrible Shipping
reviews.body: Over the past 8 years I have used numerous portable bidets/showers and this brand/product has been the best so far in terms of durability and quality. I own two bidet showers at home, which my family uses daily and they both arr 3 years old and are still working great.But recently I ordered a 3rd new one, and it never arrived even after a month. It was marked for 2 day delivery! After 4 delays over 4 weeks, and 3 customer service contacts, I finally decided to give up and go for a different vendor/product! Absolutely horrible shipping experience, lately and its unfortunate they are going to lose a customer, that otherwise would have been lifelong.

reviews.title: Do not expect "Niagra Falls" water pressure.
reviews.body: Worked right out of the box. Item appeared to be quality checked before shipped. Water flow was adequate to clean off puppy that likes to play in the mud. I would recommend.

reviews.title: Good purchase
reviews.body: Worked great, good quality. It worked great on our trip!

reviews.title: Amazing quality product!
reviews.body: I purchased this portable shower system for traveling. Right after I received it, I went on a trip and I was amazed by its quality and performance. It worked very well and and did the intended job, I’m happy with it.Definitely would recommend it!The only one comment I would suggest to the vendor. If you can add a collapsible bucket to the two batteries package, that would be great. I looked for a two batteries system with a bucket all together, but there was none. Either one battery with collapsible bucket, or two batteries system without the collapsible bucket. I ended up buy the collapsible bucket separately.

reviews.title: Seems to be of quality
reviews.body: The materials- while plastic seem sturdy and well constructed.  The battery was this powerful ( see video) after about an hour charge.  Nice pressure.  We will charge both batteries overnight before we take off camping.  I saw one review about needing to blow thru the hose if the draw gets sluggish due to air pockets and that did work when I switched from the shower sprayer to the pressure wash nozzle and had inadvertently lifted the pump out of the water.  After I blew from the nozzle end of the tube ( nozzle off, of course) into the water bucket, the pump worked perfectly again.  This, and a camp shower / potty tent were the last things we needed and now we have everything we need to be at home out in the woods.  I should point out that the shower nozzle just starts flowing immediately upon starting the battery whereas the sink-style pressure nozzle only flows with the lever pressed, same as your sink sprayer at home.  After we actually use it camping, i'm hoping to come back and 5 star this rating.  We may need a second one to keep in the camp kitchen, although this is very easily portable enough to use at both places.  I'll find a small plastic covered tote bin to store the sprayer kit with hose, nozzles, batteries, pump, and hanging hardware or maybe just a 5 gallon bucket which would also hold hygiene products for the "bathroom" tent  There are spare o- rings too.

reviews.title: Surprising alternative
reviews.body: I bought this to double up on a like item that I could not find from the original manufacturer. This is exactly like the previous one. The unit WORKS just like the other.  The brochure information says it puts out 0.9 gallons an hour flow rate. I believe it does better. The pressure is excellent,  nearly as strong as domestic water supply. I have used it to assist with a temporarily handicapped family member who can not bathe or shower as usual. The portability and water pressure make daily routine easy.

reviews.title: So many uses!
reviews.body: Quality is perfect

reviews.title: Perfect for camper living without having to use the tank
reviews.body: Very satisfied with this product I use it daily for showers camper life after losing everything I was looking for a way where I did not have to use the tank because I am chemical sensitive and you have to keep them cleaned with chemicals so this and a 5 gallon bucket of water has been a lifesaver for showers and dishes I've had this for a while it absolutely works perfect I plan on purchasing another just to have one in the kitchen areaThe batteries work perfect no issues at allWas iffy to purchase with the mixed reviews but so glad I did

reviews.title: Restored my quality of life after 2024 hurricanes
reviews.body: Our family was displaced by the hurricanes that hit Florida last year and we have been without hot water for months. I purchased an immersion heater along with this and now I can take hot showers again!I use the smaller nozzle, and I am able to take a full shower with less than 5 gallons of water, which is great! I feel very eco friendly!Water pressure is great (you know it needs to be charged when its not great). Having two batteries makes it so that I can just swap them out if one dies mid shower.

reviews.title: Great quality!
reviews.body: This product is amazing!  Easy to use with great water pressure. Having two batteries is great since you can charge one while still using the pump.

reviews.title: Going on year three!
reviews.body: We use it on average of 40 minutes a week for 3 years so far!  Swap out the pack once each week and recharge from my power station.Works great and as much pressure as a normal shower.  More so in some cases 😂

reviews.title: AMAZING Game Changer
reviews.body: Seriously this is the best product you may ever have!! I am no longer have any issues when staying in a hotel or travel anywhere as I have my bidet with me!! It’s amazing!! It’s great quality and work like installed bidet!

reviews.title: Does the job.
reviews.body: Good product. Had it 3 years and have no complaints.

groups.label: 水压与水流体验 · 水压满意

quotes.snippet: …now so I have gained insight as to how strong the water pressure was, tips and tricks and all . first off I was little wary of the price cuz it is a bit high. However after…
…Some complaints: I wish this pump had a built in pressure gauge that would automatically shut off the pump when the water wasn't being used. Most RV water pumps have t…
…ful ( see video) after about an hour charge. Nice pressure. We will charge both batteries overnight before we take off camping. I saw one review about needing to blow t…

…to 3 hours to water my garden and other areas with flowers etc. Now all I have to do is fill up 5gal buckets that are strategically placed throughout the garden ar…

reviews.title: Beware
reviews.body: Weak pressure.Worthless.Junk, for the price.Pour water over your head  ...

reviews.title: All around waste of money
reviews.body: The pressure isn't great, not powerful at all.  One of the batteries will no longer charge after 7 months of infrequent use. The hose has come apart multiple times.

reviews.title: Not a fan of this camp shower
reviews.body: I like concept of this camp shower. I used it to bathe my two schnauzers. The flow could've been stronger, but the battery life....OMGosh is horrible.
